section_path: High Risk Status > HR3: Third-party conformity assessment requirement
content:
## Decision question: Is your product (or the product for which your AI system is a safety component) required to undergo a third-party conformity assessment under existing EU law?

Each of the Annex I Section A product categories is governed by a specific Union harmonisation law. Some of those laws require certain products within the category to undergo a third-party conformity assessment (an assessment by an external notified body, rather than self-assessment by the manufacturer). Under Article 6 point 1, an AI system that is a safety component of, or is itself, such a product is classified as high-risk only where this third-party assessment requirement applies.

To answer this question, the relevant sectoral law for the product category must be consulted directly to determine whether a third-party conformity assessment is required for the specific product. Some of these sectoral laws permit an opt-out from third-party assessment where the conditions in Article 43(3) of the AI Act are satisfied; where the opt-out is lawfully exercised, the product is treated as not requiring third-party assessment for the purposes of this question. Organisations that are uncertain whether their product is subject to a third-party conformity assessment under the relevant sectoral law may need legal advice on the point.

If a third-party conformity assessment is required, the AI system is classified as high-risk. An organisation that is already a provider proceeds to the scope assessment (S1) carrying the high-risk classification. An organisation that is not yet a provider becomes a provider under Article 25, assumes provider obligations from this point forward, and then proceeds to the scope assessment.

If no third-party conformity assessment is required, the assessment proceeds to the Annex III test (HR4) to determine whether the system is high-risk on a different basis.

Source: Article 6 point 1, Article 43 point 3
source_document: FLI EU AI Act Compliance Checker
